title: CropGraph Beneficial Insects
description:
The fifth edge layer of the CropGraph: beneficial insects, microbes, and friend-not-foe invertebrates, with prey relationships to pest slugs, attractant relationships to plant slugs, and friend-or-foe identification context. Each entry is written for a gardener with a hand lens. `preyOn` references pest slugs validated against pest-disease.json at load time. `attractedBy` references plant slugs validated against crop-calendar.json at load time. `confusedWith` references either another beneficial slug or a pest slug, so the loader cross-references both fixtures.

Categories: `predator` (active hunters, both adult and larval stages), `parasitoid` (insects that develop inside or attached to a host, killing it), `pollinator` (bees, butterflies, moths, syrphid adults, beetle pollinators), `decomposer` (earthworms, dung beetles, pill bugs, soil arthropods that recycle organic matter), and `microbial-control` (nematodes, Bt strains, entomopathogenic fungi, biofungicides; a slight category misnomer but coherent for downstream consumption).

Evidence grounding: Xerces Society for Invertebrate Conservation, Cornell Cooperative Extension biocontrol fact sheets, UC IPM Online Natural Enemies Gallery, UF/IFAS Featured Creatures, Penn State Extension, USDA-ARS Beneficial Insects Research Unit. Identification tips prioritize visual recognition (color, shape, size, behavior) over taxonomy.

version.description: Schema version. Bump on breaking changes only.

source.description: Provenance / curation block for the whole fixture.

license.description: License under which the data is redistributed (e.g. CC-BY-4.0).

lastUpdated.description: ISO date of the last hand-curation pass.

entries.description: Beneficial entries. Order is not significant; helpers index by slug, category, prey pest slug, and attracted plant slug at load time.

slug.description: Kebab-case identifier for the beneficial. Unique within the fixture; forms its own namespace alongside crop and pest slugs.

commonName.description: Display name for the gardener. Title-case.

scientificName.description: Binomial or higher-rank Latin name. Genus capitalized; species lowercase; italicization handled at presentation time.

category.description: Functional grouping. Several entries straddle categories (adult hover flies are pollinators while larvae are predators); the category here is the dominant functional role for the listed slug.

preyOn.description: Pest or disease slugs this beneficial preys on, parasitizes, or otherwise suppresses. Validated against pest-disease.json at load time. Empty for pollinators and decomposers without a predation role.

attractedBy.description: Plant slugs that attract or support this beneficial through nectar, pollen, structural habitat, or host relationships. Validated against crop-calendar.json at load time.

habitatNeeds.description: Practical habitat requirements: nesting sites, overwintering needs, moisture, temperature window, pesticide sensitivities. Actionable for the gardener.

gardenRole.description: What this beneficial actually does in a garden, why it matters, and the quantitative scale of its effect when known (e.g. 'one larva eats 200-400 aphids before pupating').

identificationTips.description: Visual recognition cues: color, shape, size, distinctive markings, behavior. Written for a gardener with a hand lens. Concrete and specific.

confusedWith.description: Slug of another beneficial OR a pest that this beneficial is commonly confused with. Loader validates the reference exists in either beneficial-insects.json or pest-disease.json.

confusionNote.description: How to tell the two apart in the field. Diagnostic distinguishing features and behavior.

active.description: Seasons when the beneficial is active above ground or otherwise functional in the garden.

dormant.description: Seasons when the beneficial is dormant, overwintering, or otherwise absent.

seasonalPresence.description: Activity pattern across the four temperate seasons. Some entries are active year-round (greenhouse releases, soil microbes); their `dormant` array is empty.

regions.description: Where the beneficial is significant or common. `all` for ubiquitous beneficials; otherwise informal region labels (`pacific_northwest`, `eastern_north_america`, `midwest`, `south_central`, `southeast`, `southwest`, `mountain_west`, `arid`, `semi_arid`, `northern_us`, `canada`, `mild_winter`, `greenhouse`).

source.description: Citation, extension service, or research publication. Multiple sources separated by semicolons.
